text: Whole numbers: Recognise, represent and order numbers in the millions
Whole numbers: Apply place value to partition, regroup and rename numbers to 1 billion
Decimals and percentages: Recognise that the place value system can be extended beyond hundredths
Decimals and percentages: Compare, order and represent decimals
Whole numbers: Locate and represent integers on a number line
Decimals and percentages: Make connections between benchmark fractions, decimals and percentages
Decimals and percentages: Determine percentage discounts of 10%, 25% and 50%
Apply efficient mental and written strategies to solve addition and subtraction problems
Use estimation and place value understanding to determine the reasonableness of solutions
Choose and use efficient strategies to solve addition and subtraction problems
Applies known strategies to add and subtract decimals
Determine products and factors
Use partitioning and place value to multiply 2-, 3- and 4-digit numbers by one-digit numbers
Select and apply mental and written strategies to multiply 2- and 3-digit numbers by 2-digit numbers
Represent and solve division problems with whole number remainders
Select and apply strategies to divide a number with 3 or more digits by a one-digit divisor
Use estimation and rounding to check the reasonableness of answers to calculations
Select and apply strategies to solve problems involving multiplication and division with whole numbers
Multiply and divide decimals by powers of 10
Use equivalent number sentences involving multiplication and division to find unknown quantities
Represent and describe number patterns formed by multiples
Explore the use of brackets and the order of operations to write number sentences
Recognise the role of the number 1 as representing the whole
Compare and order common unit fractions
Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with the same denominator
Recognise that a fraction can represent a division
Compare common fractions with related denominators
Build up to the whole from a given fractional part
Use equivalence to add and subtract fractional quantities
Find fractional quantities of whole numbers (halves, quarters, fifths and tenths)
Position: Explore the Cartesian coordinate system
Length: Use metres and kilometres for length and distances
Length: Measure lengths to find perimeters
Angles: Estimate, measure and compare angles using degrees
Angles: Use a protractor to measure and identify types of angles
Position: Use the 4 quadrants of the coordinate plane
Length: Connect decimal representations to the metric system
Length: Convert between common metric units of length
Length: Solve problems involving the comparison of lengths using appropriate units
Angles: Investigate angles on a straight line and angles at a point
Angles: Investigate the relationships formed by the intersection of straight lines
2D shapes: Classify two-dimensional shapes and describe their properties
Area: Use hectares and square kilometres as units of measurement for area
Area: Calculate the areas of rectangles using familiar metric units
2D shapes: Dissect two-dimensional shapes and rearrange them using translations, reflections and rotations
Area: Find the area of composite figures
Area: Calculate the area of a parallelogram using subdivision and rearrangement
Area: Determine the area of a triangle
3D objects: Compare, describe and name prisms and pyramids
3D objects: Connect three-dimensional objects with two-dimensional representations
Volume: Choose appropriate units of measurement for capacity
Volume: Use displacement to investigate volumes of irregular solids
Volume: Connect decimal representations to the metric system
3D objects: Construct prisms and pyramids
Volume: Use cubic metres for measurement of volume
Volume: Recognise the multiplicative structure for finding volume
Volume: Find the volumes of rectangular prisms in cubic centimetres and cubic metres
Mass: Choose appropriate units of measurement for mass
Mass: Connect decimal representations to the metric system
Time: Compare 12- and 24-hour time systems and convert between them
Mass: Convert between common metric units of mass
Time: Solve problems involving duration, using 12- and 24-hour time
Collect categorical and discrete numerical data by observation or survey
Choose and use appropriate tables and graphs
Describe and interpret different datasets in context
Interpret and compare a range of data displays
Interpret data presented in digital media and elsewhere
List outcomes of chance experiments involving equally likely outcomes and represent probabilities
Compare observed frequencies of outcomes with expected results
Create random generators and describe probabilities using fractions
Conduct chance experiments with both small and large numbers of trials
Compare and order integers
Add and subtract positive and negative integers
Multiply and divide positive and negative integers
Apply the 4 operations to integers
Compare fractions using equivalence
Round decimals to a specified degree of accuracy using approximations
Identify terminating and recurring decimals
Identify and make use of the relationship between fractions, decimals and percentages to carry out simple conversions
Examine the concept of irrational numbers
Order and compare the value of fractions, decimals and percentages
Solve problems that involve the addition and subtraction of fractions
Solve problems that involve the multiplication and division of fractions and decimals
Represent one quantity as a fraction, decimal or percentage of another, with and without the use of digital tools
Solve problems that involve the use of percentages
Recognise and simplify ratios
Solve problems involving ratios
Recognise and simplify rates
Solve problems involving rates
Interpret and construct distance–time graphs from authentic data
Examine the concept of pronumerals as a way of representing numbers
Create algebraic expressions and evaluate them by substitution
Extend and apply the laws and properties of arithmetic to algebraic terms and expressions
Extend and apply the distributive law to the expansion of algebraic expressions
Factorise algebraic expressions by identifying numerical and algebraic factors
Apply index notation to represent whole numbers as products of powers of prime numbers
Examine cube roots and square roots
Use index notation to establish the index laws with positive-integer indices and the zero index
Solve linear equations up to 2 steps
Solve and verify linear equations by substitution
Solve quadratic equations
Plot and identify points on the Cartesian plane
Plot linear relationships on the Cartesian plane
Solve linear equations using graphical techniques
Solve problems involving the perimeter of various quadrilaterals and simple composite figures
Describe the relationships between the features of circles
Identify and define Pythagoras' theorem
Examine problems involving Pythagoras' theorem
Develop and use formulas to find the area of rectangles, triangles and parallelograms to solve problems
Develop and use the formula to find the area of circles and sectors to solve problems
Develop and use the formulas to find the area of trapeziums, rhombuses and kites to solve problems
Choose appropriate units of measurement for area and convert between units
Describe the different views of prisms and solids that have been formed from prism combinations
Develop and apply the formula to find the volume of a prism to solve problems
Develop the formula for finding the volume of a cylinder and apply the formula to solve problems
Choose appropriate units of measurement for volume and capacity and convert between units
Apply the language, notation and conventions of geometry
Identify geometrical properties of angles at a point
Identify and describe corresponding, alternate and co-interior angles when 2 straight lines are crossed by a transversal, including parallel lines
Solve numerical problems involving angles using reasoning
Classify triangles according to their side and angle properties
Classify quadrilaterals and describe their properties
Apply the properties of triangles and quadrilaterals
Classify data as either numerical (discrete or continuous) or categorical (nominal or ordinal) variables
Display data using graphical representations relevant to the purpose of the data
Interpret data in graphical representations
Calculate and compare the mean, median, mode and range for simple datasets
Interpret the effect individual data points have on measures of centre and range
Analyse datasets presented in various ways and draw conclusions
Determine probabilities for chance experiments
Determine probabilities for complementary events
Solve problems involving earning money
Solve problems involving simple interest
Solve problems involving spending money
Solve problems involving compound interest and depreciation
Apply the 4 operations to simplify algebraic fractions with numerical denominators
Apply the distributive law to the expansion of algebraic expressions, and collect like terms where appropriate
Extend and apply the index laws to variables, using positive-integer indices and the zero index
Simplify algebraic products and quotients using index laws
Apply index laws to numerical expressions with negative-integer indices
Solve linear equations involving up to 3 steps
Solve linear equations involving one algebraic fraction
Solve linear equations arising from word problems and substitution into formulas
Find the midpoint and gradient of a line segment (interval) on the Cartesian plane
Find the distance between 2 points located on the Cartesian plane
Recognise and graph equations
Examine parallel, horizontal and vertical lines
Examine the gradient/slope-intercept form
Find the equations of parallel and perpendicular lines
Examine the connection between algebraic and graphical representations of quadratics and exponentials
Graph and examine quadratic relationships
Graph and examine exponential relationships
Distinguish between linear, quadratic and exponential relationships by examining their graphical representations
Identify and describe very small and very large measurements
Find absolute and percentage error
Estimate and round numbers to a specified degree of accuracy
Express numbers in scientific notation
Demonstrate and explain the constancy of trigonometric ratios for a given angle in right-angled triangles
Apply trigonometry to solve right-angled triangle problems
Solve right-angled triangle problems involving angles of elevation and depression
Solve right-angled triangle problems involving bearings
Solve problems involving areas and surface areas
Develop and apply the formula for surface areas of cylinders
Solve problems involving surface areas of cylinders and related composite solids
Solve problems involving composite solids consisting of right prisms and cylinders
Identify and describe the properties of similar figures
Solve problems using ratio and scale factors in similar figures
Examine standard deviation as a measure of spread
Determine quartiles and interquartile range
Represent datasets using box plots and use them to compare datasets
Identify and describe numerical datasets involving 2 variables
Represent datasets involving 2 numerical variables, using a scatter plot and a line of best fit, by eye
Interpret data involving 2 numerical variables, using graphical representations
Describe multistage chance experiments involving independent and dependent events
Solve problems for multistage chance experiments
Design and use simulations to model and examine situations involving probability
Identify and describe problems involving direct and inverse variation
Identify and describe graphs involving direct and inverse variation
Solve problems involving direct and inverse variation and examine the relationship between graphs and equations corresponding to proportionality
Analyse graphs that are decreasing or increasing at a constant rate
Analyse the relationship between graphs and variable rates of change
Construct graphical representations of rates of change
Apply the 4 operations involving algebraic fractions with pronumerals in the denominator
Factorise algebraic expressions by taking out a common algebraic factor
Expand binomial products and factorise monic quadratic expressions
Operate with algebraic fractions involving binomial numerators and numerical denominators
Expand, factorise and simplify algebraic expressions including special products
Apply index laws to algebraic expressions involving negative-integer indices
Describe surds
Apply knowledge of surds to solve problems
Describe and use fractional indices
Solve monic quadratic equations
Solve cubic equations
Solve linear inequalities and graph their solutions on a number line
Solve linear equations involving algebraic fractions and equations of more than 3 steps
Rearrange literal equations
Solve quadratic equations using a variety of methods
Solve linear simultaneous equations, both algebraically and graphically
Apply formulas to find the midpoint and gradient/slope of an interval on the Cartesian plane
Apply the distance formula to find the distance between 2 points located on the Cartesian plane
Use various forms of the equation of a straight line
Solve problems by applying coordinate geometry formulas
Identify line and rotational symmetries
Describe translations, reflections in an axis, and rotations through multiples of 90 degrees on the Cartesian plane, using coordinates
Graph parabolas and describe their features and transformations
Graph exponentials and describe their features and transformations
Graph hyperbolas and describe their features and transformations
Graph circles and describe their features and transformations
Distinguish between different types of graphs by examining their algebraic and graphical representations and solve problems
Graph and compare polynomial curves and describe their features and transformations
Define and operate with polynomials
Divide polynomials
Apply the factor and remainder theorems to solve problems
Graph polynomials
Examine logarithms both numerically and graphically
Establish and apply the laws of logarithms to solve problems
Define relations and functions, and use function notation
Find the domain and range of a function and graph functions
Graph regions corresponding to linear inequalities in one and 2 variables
Solve 3-dimensional problems involving right-angled triangles
Apply the sine, cosine and area rules to any triangle and solve related problems
Use the unit circle to define trigonometric functions and represent them graphically
Solve trigonometric equations using exact values and the relationships between supplementary and complementary angles
Solve problems involving surface areas
Solve problems involving volumes
Identify and explain congruence
Develop and use the conditions for congruent triangles
Develop and apply the minimum conditions for triangles to be similar
Establish and apply properties of similar shapes and solids
Apply logical reasoning to numerical problems involving plane shapes
Construct formal proofs involving congruent and similar triangles
Apply logical reasoning to proofs involving plane shapes
Prove and apply angle and chord properties of circles
Prove and apply tangent and secant properties of circles
Examine and describe a graph/network
Define a planar graph and apply Euler's formula for planar graphs
Explain the concept of Eulerian trails and circuits in the context of the Königsberg bridges problem
Plan and conduct a statistical inquiry into a question of interest
Examine reports of studies in digital media and elsewhere for information on their planning and implementation
Solve problems involving Venn diagrams and 2-way tables
Use the language, 'if ... then', 'given', 'of' and 'knowing that', to examine conditional statements and identify common mistakes in interpreting the language
Describe mutually and non-mutually exclusive events using specific language and calculate related probabilities
Substitute into algebraic expressions, equations and formulas and evaluate the subject of a formula
Calculate distance, speed and time, and stopping distances of vehicles
Apply formulas to calculate and interpret blood alcohol content (BAC)
Calculate medication dosages using Fried's, Young's and Clark's formulas
Change the subject of formulas and model word problems as linear equations
Determine the gradient, y-intercept and equation of a straight line and construct its graph
Model and interpret linear relationships in context, including interpolation, extrapolation and limitations
Recognise, represent and graph direct variation of the form $y = kx$
Calculate earnings from salaries, wages, overtime, commission, piecework and royalties
Calculate earnings over different pay periods and leave loading
Determine taxable income, tax payable, PAYG tax and the Medicare levy
Calculate net pay and determine tax refunds or amounts owing
Apply percentages to profit, loss and GST when purchasing goods
Compare payment and purchasing options including buy now pay later and buying on terms
Calculate the costs of purchasing and running a vehicle
Calculate household costs and prepare a personal budget
Convert between metric units of length, area, volume, capacity and mass
Apply scientific notation and standard prefixes, with significant figures
Calculate perimeters and areas of standard, composite and irregular shapes using Pythagoras' theorem
Solve problems involving the surface area of prisms, cylinders, spheres and composite solids
Solve problems involving the volume and capacity of prisms, cylinders, spheres, cones, pyramids and composite solids
Apply the Trapezoidal rule to approximate areas
Identify a location on Earth using latitude, longitude and position coordinates
Calculate time differences between locations using longitude
Convert between units of time and time formats and calculate elapsed time
Solve problems involving time zones, daylight saving and UTC
Use network terminology and construct network diagrams from tables or maps
Solve problems involving network diagrams in practical contexts
Determine minimum spanning trees and solve minimal connector problems
Identify the shortest path on a network diagram
Pose statistical questions and design surveys, considering bias and ethics
Compare sampling methods and evaluate whether a sample is representative
Classify data as numerical or categorical, discrete or continuous, nominal or ordinal
Represent grouped and ungrouped data using appropriate graphs
Describe the shape of a distribution and interpret a range of data displays
Calculate and compare measures of centre and spread, including standard deviation
Determine the five-number summary and interquartile range and represent data using box plots
Identify clusters, gaps and outliers and the impact of outliers on measures
Solve simultaneous linear equations graphically and algebraically and model practical situations
Use simultaneous equations to model break-even points, profit and loss
Graph and model exponential growth and decay relationships
Graph quadratic relationships and identify their key features
Interpret and apply quadratic models in context
Graph reciprocal relationships and solve inverse variation problems
Calculate simple interest and compare simple interest graphs
Calculate compound interest, including inflation and appreciation
Compare the growth of simple and compound interest
Interpret share values and calculate dividends, yield and brokerage
Apply straight-line and declining balance methods of depreciation
Model and solve problems involving reducing balance loans
Interpret credit cards as reducing balance loans and calculate interest
Identify and model an annuity in tabular form
Use tables of interest factors to calculate annuity values and contributions
Solve annuity problems involving superannuation, savings and loans
Apply trigonometric ratios to right-angled triangles
Apply the sine rule to find unknown sides and angles, including the obtuse case
Apply the cosine rule to find unknown sides and angles
Apply the area rule for triangles
Solve practical problems involving bearings, radial surveys and triangle trigonometry
Simplify ratios, divide quantities in a ratio and solve practical ratio problems
Apply ratios to map scales, scale drawings and the capture–recapture technique
Calculate area and rainfall volume from scaled plans, surveys and maps
Represent, convert and compare rates, including best buys
Interpret distance–time graphs and examine fuel and energy consumption
Use network flow terminology and convert tables to flow networks
Calculate flow capacity using the maximum-flow minimum-cut theorem
Analyse whether a network's flow capacity meets demand
Construct a network diagram from a precedence table, including dummy activities
Apply forward and backward scanning to find EST, LST and float times
Determine the critical path and minimum completion time
Construct a Gantt chart and examine the effect of changing activity durations
Identify bivariate data and distinguish correlation from causation
Represent bivariate data with a scatter plot and describe the association
Fit a line of best fit, find its equation and make predictions
Calculate Pearson's correlation coefficient and the least-squares regression line
Determine sample spaces and calculate probabilities of events and their complements
Use arrays, tree diagrams and probability trees for multistage events
Use relative frequency and expected frequency to estimate probability
Construct and interpret Venn diagrams and two-way tables
Recognise normally distributed data and the bell-shaped curve
Calculate, interpret and compare z-scores
Apply the empirical rule for normally distributed data
Use z-scores and the empirical rule to calculate probabilities and make judgements
Algebraic techniques: Use index laws and expand, factorise and simplify algebraic expressions and algebraic fractions
Algebraic techniques: Expand, simplify and rationalise expressions involving surds, including binomial surd denominators
Algebraic techniques: Solve quadratic equations and use the discriminant to determine the number and nature of the roots
Introduction to functions and relations: Define relations and functions, use function notation, and identify domain, range and zeroes
Linear functions: Determine and graph equations of straight lines, including parallel and perpendicular lines, and solve linear inequalities
Quadratic and cubic functions: Graph parabolas and cubics, solve quadratic inequalities and simultaneous equations, and equate coefficients
Reciprocal functions: Graph $f(x)=k/x$ and describe its behaviour as $x$ approaches infinity
Constructing and using functions: Model and solve practical problems using linear, simultaneous and quadratic functions, including break-even analysis
Direct and inverse variation: Develop and apply the models $y=kx^{n}$ and $y=k/x^{n}$
Circles and semicircles: Derive and graph $x^{2}+y^{2}=r^{2}$ and the related semicircles
Properties of functions, relations and graphs: Use interval notation for domain and range, even and odd functions, and composite functions
Piecewise-defined functions: Interpret and graph piecewise functions and identify continuity and discontinuities
Absolute value functions: Define, graph and solve equations involving the absolute value
Trigonometry with acute angles: Find exact ratios for $30^{\circ}$, $45^{\circ}$ and $60^{\circ}$ and solve right-angled triangle problems, including bearings and elevation and depression
Trigonometry with angles of any magnitude: Define and use the trigonometric ratios on a circle for angles of any magnitude using related angles
Trigonometry with angles of any magnitude: Examine and apply the sine rule, cosine rule and area of a triangle, including the ambiguous case
Radians: Define radian measure, convert between degrees and radians, find exact ratios for multiples of $\tfrac{\pi}{6}$ and $\tfrac{\pi}{4}$, and graph the trigonometric functions
Radians: Use $l=r\theta$ and $A=\tfrac{1}{2}r^{2}\theta$ to solve problems involving arc lengths, sectors and segments
Define the reciprocal trigonometric functions $\sec\theta$, $\operatorname{cosec}\theta$ and $\cot\theta$ and find their exact values
Establish and use the reciprocal and quotient identities, identifying excluded angles
Prove and use the complementary angle identities
Prove and use the Pythagorean identities
Solve trigonometric equations, including those that reduce to quadratics, on a restricted domain
Apply trigonometric identities to simplify expressions and prove further identities
Estimating change: Define and calculate the average rate of change as the gradient of a secant
Estimating change: Relate the instantaneous rate of change to the gradient of a tangent and estimate it from a graph
The derivative: Approximate the gradient of a curve and infer the derivative of $x^{n}$
The derivative: Define f'(x) as the gradient of the tangent and differentiate from first principles
Calculations with the derivative: Use the power rule and the sum and multiple rules to differentiate
Calculations with the derivative: Find equations of tangents and normals and relate gradient to the angle of inclination
Calculations with the derivative: Apply the product, quotient and chain rules
Graphical applications of the derivative: Use the sign of f'(x) to find increasing, decreasing and stationary points and graph the derivative
The derivative as a rate of change: Interpret f'(c) as an instantaneous rate of change and apply it to motion in a straight line
Exponential functions: Graph $y=k(a^{x})$ and $y=k(a^{-x})$, identifying the asymptote, intercept, domain, range and behaviour
Exponential functions: Identify Euler's number $e$ and recognise that $e^{x}$ is its own derivative
Logarithmic functions: Define logarithms and the natural logarithm and evaluate exponential and logarithmic expressions
Logarithmic functions: Solve exponential equations and use the logarithmic properties and laws to simplify, solve and prove results
Logarithmic functions: Graph $y=\log_a x$ and recognise it as the reflection of $y=a^{x}$ in the line $y=x$
Establish reflections in the $x$- and $y$-axes from replacing $x$ by $-x$ and $y$ by $-y$
Establish horizontal and vertical translations from replacing $x$ by $x-a$ and $y$ by $y-b$
Establish horizontal and vertical dilations and enlargements from replacing $x$ by $x/k$ and $y$ by $y/l$
Apply reflections, translations and dilations to functions and determine the rule, graph, domain, range and key features
Use translations to find the centre, radius, domain and range of circles, including by completing the square
Recognise that the order in which transformations are applied is important
Sets and set notation: Use set notation for elements, subsets, intersection, union and complement, and apply $|A\cup B|=|A|+|B|-|A\cap B|$
Probability: Define experiments, sample spaces and events and calculate probabilities of equally likely outcomes
Probability: Use the complement and addition rules and arrays and tree diagrams for multistage events
Conditional probability: Define and calculate conditional probability and test for independence
Data: Distinguish discrete and continuous random variables and organise data using frequency tables
Data: Graph frequency, relative frequency and cumulative frequency, and estimate probabilities from relative frequencies
Transformations of trigonometric functions: Apply reflections, translations and dilations to the trigonometric functions and determine amplitude, period, domain and range
Transformations of trigonometric functions: Solve equations and graphical problems involving transformed trigonometric functions within a domain
Modelling with functions: Model periodic phenomena using transformations of the trigonometric functions
Modelling with functions: Model and solve practical problems using functions and their transformations
Modelling with functions: Use logarithmic scales to model and solve problems
Sequences and series: Use notation for sequences, terms, partial sums, series and summation (sigma) notation
Arithmetic sequences and series: Use $a_{n}=a+(n-1)d$ and the partial sum formulas for an arithmetic sequence
Arithmetic sequences and series: Apply arithmetic sequences and series to model linear growth and decay
Geometric sequences and series: Use $a_{n}=ar^{n-1}$ and the sum formula for a geometric sequence
Geometric sequences and series: Find the limiting sum of a geometric series with |r|<1
Geometric sequences and series: Apply geometric sequences and series to model exponential growth and decay
Differentiation with exponential functions: Differentiate $ke^{ax}$, $e^{f(x)}$ and $a^{x}$
Differentiation with logarithmic functions: Differentiate $\ln x$, $\log_a x$ and $\ln f(x)$
Differentiation with trigonometric functions: Differentiate sin, cos, tan, sec, cosec and cot and their compositions
Using derivatives: Apply the product, quotient and chain rules to functions within the course, including tangents and normals
Primitive functions: Find primitives of power functions and $(ax+b)^{n}$ and use initial conditions
The definite integral: Interpret the definite integral as a limit of sums and evaluate it using areas
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: Recognise and apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Indefinite integrals: Use indefinite integral notation and the reverse chain rule for power functions
Integration with exponential functions: Integrate $e^{x}$, $e^{ax+b}$, $a^{x}$ and $e^{f(x)}f'(x)$
Integration with logarithmic functions: Integrate $\dfrac{1}{x}$, $\dfrac{1}{ax+b}$ and $\dfrac{f'(x)}{f(x)}$
Integration with trigonometric functions: Integrate sin, cos, $\sec^{2}$ and related composite forms
Areas and the definite integral: Calculate areas between curves and the x- or y-axis, using symmetry and the Trapezoidal rule
Turning points, inflections and curve-sketching: Relate differentiability and continuity and find second derivatives
Turning points, inflections and curve-sketching: Classify stationary points and use concavity and points of inflection
Turning points, inflections and curve-sketching: Sketch curves using calculus and graph f'(x) and f''(x)
Optimisation: Model and solve optimisation problems using calculus, considering domains and endpoints
Rates of change: Use first and second derivatives and integration to solve rate-of-change problems
Rates of change: Model exponential growth and decay using $\dfrac{dQ}{dt}=kQ$ and $Q=Ae^{kt}$
Rates of change: Connect displacement, velocity and acceleration using calculus
Discrete random variables: Define discrete probability distributions and the uniform distribution
Discrete random variables: Calculate the expected value, variance and standard deviation of a discrete random variable
Continuous random variables: Use probability density functions and cumulative distribution functions
Continuous random variables: Find the mean, variance, median and quartiles of a continuous random variable
The normal distribution: Identify the normal distribution and its features and apply the empirical rule
The normal distribution: Use z-scores to compare values and to find probabilities
Reducing balance loans: Recognise a reducing balance loan as compound interest with periodic repayments and solve related problems
Annuities: Identify annuities and model the future value of an annuity using the sum of a geometric sequence
Graphical relationships: Graph $y=\dfrac{1}{f(x)}$, reciprocal trig functions, $y=|f(x)|$, $y=f(|x|)$ and sums and differences of functions, finding their domains and ranges
Inverse functions: Identify one-to-one functions, find and graph inverse functions, relate their domains and ranges, and solve related problems
Parametric form of a function or relation: Express, convert and graph linear and quadratic functions and circles in parametric form
Inequalities: Solve cubic, rational and absolute value inequalities
Language and graphs of polynomials: Define polynomials and their features, zeroes, roots and multiplicity, end behaviour, and graph from factored form
Remainder and factor theorems: Divide polynomials, express the result as $P(x)=A(x)Q(x)+R(x)$, and prove and apply the remainder and factor theorems
Sums and products of zeroes of polynomials: Relate the zeroes of quadratic, cubic and quartic polynomials to their coefficients and solve problems
Trigonometry in three dimensions: Interpret 3D contexts and apply trigonometry to solve problems in three dimensions
Further trigonometric identities: Derive and use the sum, difference and double angle formulas
Further trigonometric equations: Solve trig equations using identities and the auxiliary angle form $a\cos x + b\sin x = R\cos(x\pm\alpha)$
Factorials and the multiplication principle: Use factorial notation and the multiplication principle to count arrangements of distinct objects
Permutations: Use $^{n}P_{r}$ for ordered selections, including objects that are not all distinct and arrangements with restrictions
Circular arrangements: Count circular arrangements of distinct objects with and without restrictions
Combinations: Define and use $^{n}C_{r}$, establish its identities, and solve problems involving combinations and cases
Applications to probability: Solve probability problems using permutations and combinations
Pascal's triangle and binomial coefficients: Build Pascal's triangle and relate $^{n}C_{r}$ to the coefficient of $x^{n-r}y^{r}$
The binomial theorem: Expand $(x+y)^{n}$ and find a specific term or coefficient
Identities involving binomial coefficients: Simplify expressions and prove identities using binomial coefficients
Proof by mathematical induction: Examine the structure of inductive proof and prove results for sums and divisibility, identifying errors in false proofs
Vector representation and notation: Define vectors and their magnitude and direction, and represent vectors graphically
Introduction to 2D and 3D vectors: Represent points and vectors in 2D and 3D in component, ordered-pair/triple and column form, and use the distance and midpoint formulas
Operating with vectors: Multiply by a scalar, add and subtract vectors, test for parallel vectors, and find magnitudes and unit vectors
Further operations with vectors: Use the scalar (dot) product to find angles, test perpendicularity and find projections
Motion in vector form in two dimensions: Describe position, velocity and acceleration as vector functions of time and solve motion problems
Projectile motion: Model projectile motion in vector form and determine features of the flight
Definitions of inverse trigonometric functions: Restrict the domains of sin, cos and tan to define arcsin, arccos and arctan and evaluate expressions
Graphs of inverse trigonometric functions: Graph inverse trig functions, classify parity, prove properties, analyse composites and apply transformations
Further derivatives of functions: Differentiate parametric, inverse and inverse trigonometric functions and combinations of functions
Techniques of integration: Integrate inverse-trig forms, use integration by substitution, and integrate $\sin^{2}nx$ and $\cos^{2}nx$
Multiplicity of zeroes of polynomial functions: Use P'(x) to determine multiplicity, relate multiplicity to graph behaviour and graph polynomials using calculus
Further rates of change: Solve related rates problems and model quantities whose rate of change is proportional to $Q-P$
Areas between curves and volumes of solids of revolution: Calculate areas between curves and volumes of revolution about either axis
Differential equations: Define differential equations, use slope fields and solve first order differential equations including by separation of variables
Bernoulli distributions: Define and model a Bernoulli random variable and find its mean and variance
Binomial distributions: Define a binomial random variable, use $P(X=r)=\,^{n}C_{r}\,p^{r}(1-p)^{n-r}$, and find expected frequencies, mean and variance
Sampling distribution of the mean and the central limit theorem: Define the sampling distribution of the mean and apply the central limit theorem
The language and notation of proof: Use the formal language, logical connectives, quantifiers and negation of statements, and the converse and contrapositive
Illustrations of proofs: Use proof by contradiction, examples and counterexamples, and prove results involving integers
Proof of inequalities: Prove inequalities using definitions, non-negative squares, order properties, the triangle inequality, the AM–GM inequality, the squeeze theorem and calculus
Further proof by mathematical induction: Prove identities, inequalities, calculus and geometric results, and results involving recurrence relations, using induction
Vector equations of lines and curves: Use r=a+λb for lines in 2D and 3D, test parallelism, collinearity, intersection and skewness, and express curves, circles and spheres as vector equations
Vectors and geometry: Use properties of the dot product, prove the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality, and prove geometric results using vectors
Arithmetic of complex numbers: Define i, the complex numbers, real and imaginary parts, the conjugate and modulus, and add, subtract, multiply, divide and take square roots
Geometric representation of complex numbers: Plot complex numbers, use argument, principal argument and polar form, and prove modulus, argument and conjugate identities
Solving equations with complex numbers: Solve quadratics with complex coefficients and prove and apply the complex conjugate root theorem
Powers and roots of complex numbers: Prove and use de Moivre’s theorem, find nth roots of ±1 and of complex numbers, and use complex numbers as vectors
Describing lines, curves and regions: Graph lines, circles, rays and regions defined using complex numbers
Trigonometric products as sums and differences: Derive and use the product-to-sum identities to solve equations and evaluate integrals of products
The t-formulas: Derive sinA, cosA and tanA in terms of t=tan(A/2) and use them to solve trigonometric equations
Integration by substitution: Find indefinite and evaluate definite integrals by substitution, where the substitution may or may not be given
Partial fractions: Decompose and integrate rational functions using partial fraction decomposition
Integrating rational functions: Integrate by completing the square, for improper rational functions, and by algebraic manipulation of the integrand
Integration by parts: Derive and use integration by parts, including repeated application and recurrence relations
Combining techniques of integration: Solve theoretical and practical problems requiring multiple techniques of integration
Forces and further motion in a straight line: Use the acceleration expressions dv/dt, v(dv/dx) and d/dx(½v²), apply Newton’s laws and F=m­¨x, and resolve forces into components
Simple harmonic motion: Define SHM by ¨x=−n²(x−c), verify solutions, derive v²=n²[A²−(x−c)²], and graph and solve SHM problems
Modelling motion without resistance: Derive and use the equations of motion, including on smooth inclined planes and with a single smooth pulley
Rectilinear resisted motion: Derive acceleration, velocity and displacement for a particle resisted by a force proportional to a power of the speed, and solve problems
Vertical resisted motion: Derive motion equations under gravity with resistance, define and determine terminal velocity, and solve problems
Projectiles and resisted motion: Compare trajectories with and without resistance and establish the equations of motion for a projectile resisted proportionally to the speed
